title: Implement `bead claims --plain` CLI Mode
description: Extend `orchestrator bead claims` to accept `--plain` and render one compact line per active claim in the required field order, while keeping JSON output unchanged when `--plain` is absent.
acceptance_criteria: `orchestrator bead claims` output remains JSON-compatible with current behavior.
`orchestrator bead claims --plain` emits one line per claim formatted as `bead_id | agent_type | feature=<feature_root_id> | lease=<lease_owner>`.
When no active claims exist in plain mode, output is exactly `No active claims.`.
No claim selection/filtering/sorting logic is changed.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented `format_claims_plain()` in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py`; added `--plain` argument to `bead claims`; updated `command_bead` claims branch to emit plain text in required format (`bead_id | agent_type | feature=<feature_root_id> | lease=<lease_owner>`) and `No active claims.` when empty; retained existing `storage.active_claims()` path and ordering logic.
handoff_summary.remaining: No code changes remaining for this bead.
handoff_summary.risks: No dedicated automated test for `bead claims --plain` exists in current suite. I validated nearby CLI behavior with existing tests, but plain-mode behavior is currently validated by implementation inspection rather than a committed test.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to review agent for verification and optional test coverage extension for `bead claims --plain`.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Potential merge conflict if sibling work edits `build_parser()` or `command_bead()` in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` around `bead` subparser definitions and claims handling.

conflict_risks: Potential merge conflict if sibling work edits `build_parser()` or `command_bead()` in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` around `bead` subparser definitions and claims handling.
